text: {"fixed_xml":"<ITEM>In denen Thurneysischen Buchläden allhier werden die ersten zwey Theile von folgendem Werck denen Herren Subseribenten ausgeliffert: <BIBL><AUTHOR>Roberti Stephani</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Thesaurus Linguae Latinae post noviss. Londin. edit. insignitor auctam Annokationibus Honrici Stephani Locupletatus</TITLE>. Nova cura retensuit digessit &c. <AUTHOR>Ant. Birrius</AUTHOR>. <FORMAT>fol.</FORMAT></BIBL> Ingleichen geben sie auch den <BIBL><TITLE>T. Lieium e. Integr. Sigonii & Gronovior Norit</TITLE> in <VOLUME>3. Voll.</VOLUME> auf schön Schreib-Median- Pappier in <FORMAT>8.</FORMAT> à <PRIZE>fl. 6.</PRIZE></BIBL> künfftige Wochen an die Herren Liebhaber aus, dieser Preiß aber währet nur biß Ende Januarii <YEAR>1740</YEAR>. nach welcher Zeit kein Eremplar unter <PRIZE>7fl. 30. xr.</PRIZE> verkaufft werden solle.</ITEM>","number_of_corrections":10,"explanation":"1) Replaced corrupted encoding \"Buchl√§den\" with \"Buchläden\". 2) Replaced \"ausgel√∫ffert\" with \"ausgeliffert\". 3) Escaped the literal ampersand in \"&c.\" to \"&c.\" (twice — also in \"Sigonii & Gronovior\" → \"Sigonii & Gronovior\"). 4) Replaced \"sch√∂n\" with \"schön\". 5) Replaced the stray symbol \"√†\" with \"à\" (the abbreviation for the price). 6) Closed the second BIBL element, which was left open: the price information belongs to the bibliographic entry, so I placed the closing </BIBL> tag after the FORMAT/PRIZE group. 7) Replaced \"k√∫nfftige\" with \"künfftige\". 8) Replaced \"Prei√º\" with \"Preiß\". 9) Replaced \"w√§hret\" with \"währet\". 10) Replaced \"bi√º\" with \"biß\". These corrections restore the German special characters mangled by faulty encoding, escape XML-illegal ampersands, and close the second BIBL tag so the markup is well-formed."}

parsed.explanation: 1) Replaced corrupted encoding "Buchl√§den" with "Buchläden". 2) Replaced "ausgel√∫ffert" with "ausgeliffert". 3) Escaped the literal ampersand in "&c." to "&c." (twice — also in "Sigonii & Gronovior" → "Sigonii & Gronovior"). 4) Replaced "sch√∂n" with "schön". 5) Replaced the stray symbol "√†" with "à" (the abbreviation for the price). 6) Closed the second BIBL element, which was left open: the price information belongs to the bibliographic entry, so I placed the closing </BIBL> tag after the FORMAT/PRIZE group. 7) Replaced "k√∫nfftige" with "künfftige". 8) Replaced "Prei√º" with "Preiß". 9) Replaced "w√§hret" with "währet". 10) Replaced "bi√º" with "biß". These corrections restore the German special characters mangled by faulty encoding, escape XML-illegal ampersands, and close the second BIBL tag so the markup is well-formed.
